# Screener Weekly — 2026-05-30

*Generated by the Saturday-morning routine. Source data: live `/api/screener/morning/run` against the production Railway deployment.*

## TL;DR

**API failure — no trade data available this week (fifth consecutive week).** Both the screener run endpoint (`POST /api/screener/morning/run`) and the universe endpoint (`GET /api/screener/universe`) returned HTTP 403 with the body `Host not in allowlist`. This failure has now occurred on 2026-04-26, 2026-05-02, 2026-05-16, 2026-05-23, and 2026-05-30. The Railway deployment's host allowlist is blocking the claude.ai/code routines execution environment. No ticker data was generated; nothing in this report should be used for Monday trade prep until the API access issue is resolved.

## API Failure Details

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Endpoint | `POST /api/screener/morning/run` |
| HTTP Status | 403 |
| Response Body | `Host not in allowlist` |
| Universe Endpoint | `GET /api/screener/universe?mode=swing` |
| Universe HTTP Status | 403 |
| Universe Response Body | `Host not in allowlist` |
| Note | Fifth consecutive Saturday blocked — 2026-04-26, 2026-05-02, 2026-05-16, 2026-05-23, 2026-05-30 |

## Caveats / known gaps

- **API blocked (403) — fifth consecutive week:** The production Railway deployment at `https://trend-trading-mcp-production.up.railway.app` returned `Host not in allowlist` for both screener endpoints. The claude.ai/code routines runner's outbound IP needs to be added to the Railway deployment's allowlist, or the allowlist restriction needs to be removed/relaxed for authenticated bearer-token requests.
- Plan 1 only ships Coiled Spring — other scans (Pradeep 4%, Qullamaggie EP, Saty Triggers, Vomy, etc.) are deferred to Plan 2.
- `backfill_days_in_compression` is not yet wired into the runner — newly-detected coils start at day 1 even if they've been coiled for weeks. Plan 2 task.
- The compression proxy (rolling stddev/SMA20) is a stand-in for the real Phase Oscillator. Plan 2 will replace it.

## Action required (escalated — five consecutive failures)

The allowlist fix recommended on 2026-04-26, 2026-05-02, 2026-05-16, and 2026-05-23 has still not been applied. This is the fifth consecutive week of failure. The Saturday screener routine has produced zero trade-actionable data since it was set up. To unblock next Saturday's run, either:

1. **Remove the host allowlist restriction** and rely solely on the bearer token for authentication (preferred — the routine runner uses dynamic IPs that rotate between sessions).
2. **Add the claude.ai/code routines execution environment IPs** to the Railway deployment's allowlist (fragile if IPs rotate, which they do).

Option 1 is strongly preferred. The bearer token provides sufficient authentication; the host allowlist adds no meaningful security and is actively breaking the Saturday automation.

**Suggested Railway fix:** In your Railway service settings, locate the `ALLOWED_HOSTS` or equivalent middleware configuration. If it is checking `req.hostname` or `req.headers.host` against an allowlist, either remove that check entirely (for a bearer-token-protected API) or add a wildcard bypass for authenticated requests.
